behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in segmentation logic itself (single parser/splitter boundary). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text only asks for segmentation semantics, not cross-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test the segmentation function with a multi-line shell block; assert output segments are per statement line (assignments and command split), not a single combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real incident came from interaction between segmentation and trigger classification. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required to reproduce classification miss, (b) no AI/ML output dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about parsing/classifying command text, not executing `gh`.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring segmenter + trigger detector on a newline-separated block ending in `gh pr create`; assert final classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: negative case depends on same segmentation->classification boundary as behavior 2. Integration-brake: no real subprocess/network needed because command is inspected, not executed; no AI dependency; no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: rejected System due to lack of behavior text requiring real `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with multiline assignments + `gh issue create`; assert classifier does not emit PR-create trigger and returns the expected non-trigger class.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is detector output -> identifier reconstruction -> persistence adapter. Integration-brake: (a) behavior requires persistence, but not OS-specific events/process behavior; local temp filesystem/module wiring is sufficient, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text asks for reconstruction/persistence format, not real external API/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that feeds a detected PR-create event payload through the hook pipeline, then asserts persisted state contains the reconstructed PR identifier fields expected by the review loop.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an inter-module contract test (PostToolUse writer and pre-tool reader/enforcer). Integration-brake: (a) no requirement for real network/subprocess/OS event semantics, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about state compatibility between hooks, which is reproducible in-process with local files.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that runs post-tool state write, then invokes pre-tool enforcement read path against that state and asserts enforcement decision is activated for the PR context.
